area: restart.md not copied into project prompts dir
finding: When ocman writes a *.restart.md, it does not also drop a copy into the working project's .agents/prompts/pending/ even when that project uses an .agents/plans|prompts convention. The user wants an auto-copy so restart docs land where their agent workflows expect them.
proposed_action: After writing the restart file, if the target project dir contains .agents/plans/ OR .agents/prompts/, copy the restart file to <project>/.agents/prompts/pending/YYYYMMDD-<session_id>.restart.md (create pending/ if missing).

area: which directory is 'the project being worked on' is ambiguous
finding: Recovery has THREE candidate dirs: CWD, --session-dir (opencode_cwd), and output_dir (default ./opencode-recovery). The request says 'the project (repo) being worked on'. Choosing wrong could write into an unintended repo.
disposition: Define the target as: --session-dir if given, else the session's DB directory (session.raw['directory']) if it exists on disk, else CWD. Confirm precedence with user (open question). Only act if that dir actually contains .agents/plans or .agents/prompts.

area: YYYYMMDD must come from session last-updated (epoch ms as string
evidence: session.updated is str(time_updated) epoch-ms and can be 'unknown'. Naive use would produce a bad filename.
disposition: Derive YYYYMMDD by parsing session.updated as epoch-ms (reuse the _fmt_ts date logic); if unavailable/'unknown', fall back to the process startup date (get_startup_timestamp_local) so the filename is always valid; note the fallback.

area: requested backup scheme differs from existing _backup_if_exists
finding: Request: on name collision, back up existing to *.restart.bu.###.md (### from 001). ocman's _backup_if_exists uses .NN.bak (2-digit from 01) — a different scheme, and it RENAMES (moves) rather than keeping the new file at the canonical name.
proposed_action: Implement the requested scheme as a small dedicated helper: before writing the new copy, if the target exists, rename it to <name>.restart.bu.001.md (next free ### >= 001, zero-padded to 3), then write the new file at the canonical name. Do NOT reuse _backup_if_exists (different scheme).

finding: session_id is used in the filename; must be filesystem-safe (session ids are ses_ + alnum but validate).
proposed_action: Use safe_filename(session.session_id) for the filename component, consistent with existing recovery filenames.

area: path-escape + write safety into another repo
finding: Copying into <project>/.agents/prompts/pending/ writes into a DIRECTORY THE USER MAY NOT OWN/expect; must not follow symlinks out, must not overwrite unrelated files, and must fail soft (a copy failure must not break the primary recovery output).
proposed_action: Resolve the target path and assert it stays under <project>/.agents/prompts/pending/; wrap the whole copy in try/except so any failure only warns and never aborts the main restart/transcript write; do not create .agents itself, only pending/ under an existing .agents/prompts (or .agents) dir.

finding: Auto-writing into the repo is a side effect; a user may want to disable it, and should be told it happened.
proposed_action: Print an [INFO] line naming the copied path; add a config key (e.g. copy_restart_to_project_prompts, default true) and/or a --no-project-prompt flag to opt out. Keep default on (matches the request).

finding: Request is specifically about *.restart.md (not transcript/prompt/compacted). Scope creep risk.
proposed_action: Copy ONLY the restart file. Do not copy transcript/prompt/compacted. (If the user later wants the LLM-compacted output too, that's a separate decision.)
